feedback.text: Today, when buying video games you have only 2 options, physical version and digital version, both options do not give you ownership of a video game if not a usage licence, this means that you will be able to enjoy the purchased product until the company allows it. However, in music and films, if you buy it, I am. If you buy a product, you must belong to you. A company cannot oblige you to pay for a temporary licence, which can then be arbitrarily deactivated. In addition, the user needs to be shielded from updates. Nowadays, all video games receive updates that change the product to good or bad, if I have paid for a product with the characteristics A, B and C and receive a mandatory update that changes A to be D no longer playing the product you paid for, and they should continue to benefit fully from the original A B C without you being obliged to switch to D B C. This does not pass on other markets when you buy a pellet in any format that is not vague in time and is always available for being treated as a product. Both are entertainment products that you have legally purchased by paying a price for them. One behaves differently from the rest. Another important point is that these products mostly include micro payments that unlock content that the product you bought in. When buying a product with micro payments, companies should be obliged to inform you that you get with the basic product and that it contains micro payments. Moreover, another important point to limit is the use of intermediate coins in micro payments. All games have an X currency with which all the content of micro transactions is purchased with real money and can only be purchased in packs. Example EUR 5 = 100 store points, with this premise if your consumer wants a painting for your character and costs 150 points, forcing you to buy it with currency X so you have to put a higher amount than you want to buy the product. This creates 2 problems. The first is the dissociation of the real money you pay when you always use currency X and the second is that when you have done to pay 2 packs when you only need 1.5 you leave you a balance sheet of EUR 2,5 that you can no longer recover because you have exchanged them for points that you don’t need what makes in the future you need more money for another purchase than you would otherwise not have done.
